other_observers: none; John Breitsch discovered the reported the bird on November 21 (http://birding.aba.org/message.php?mesid=287486&MLID=CO01&MLNM=Colorado), and he obtained some excellent photos (http://www.flickr.com/photos/breitschbirding/8206673930/in/photostream).
specific_location: Walden Ponds, junipers on northwest side of Ricky Weiser pond 40.05 deg N, 105.19 deg W
description: large sparrow with a large yellow bill, brightly-colored rufous and gray head (distinguishing this bird from slate-colored fox sparrow), rufous wings with faint white wing bars, rufous caret-shaped spots on otherwise white breast and flanksBehaviors: This fox sparrow had been reported by John Breitsch on Nov. 21 (http://birding.aba.org/message.php?mesid=287486&MLID=CO01&MLNM=Colorado), and I found it (presumably the same bird) where it had been reported previously. The bird was in thick junipers, and I located it by hearing it moving. It mostly stayed out of sight and silent deep within the junipers. As a hiker walked by on the opposite side of the junipers, the sparrow briefly peered out on the side where I was standing, in time for me to get a brief look through binoculars and one photo. It then resumed hiding within the junipers.Call: none heardPlumage: adult
